name: RootHelper
summary:
RootHelper
Roothelper will aid in the process of privilege escalation on a Linux system that has been compromised. The latest version totals eleven scripts. From enumeration to exploit suggestion to exploit deployment. RootHelper ensures you have access to the best tools for the job.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Index entry in the Penetration Testing category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.
Pairs with the Penetration Testing system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.
Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.
Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.
Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.
